questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A buffer solution contains 0.2 mole of acetic acid and 0.25 mole of potassium acetate per litre. Calculate the change of pH of the solution if 0.5 ml of 1 M HCl is added to it. The $\mathrm{pK}_{\mathrm{a}}$ of $\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COOH}$ is 4.76. The volume change on the addition of HCl may be neglected.
questions.solution:
pH of the solution before adding HCl
$\mathrm{pH}=\mathrm{pK}_{\mathrm{a}}+\log \frac{0.25}{0.20}=4.8569$
HCl added $=0.0005$ mole
After adding HCl
$\begin{aligned}
{\left[\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COOH}\right] } & =0.20+0.0005=0.2005 \\
{\left[\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COOK}\right] } & =0.25-0.0005=0.2495
\end{aligned}$
$\mathrm{pH}=4.76+\log \frac{0.2495}{0.2005}=4.8549$
Change in $\mathrm{pH}=4.8569-4.8549=0.002$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q:
Consider a 100 ml buffer with pH 2.4 prepared by mixing sodium hydrogen phosphate with 1.5 M HCl . Concentration of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{PO}_4^{-}$in buffer is 0.6 M . How much HCl (in ml ) should be added in sodium hydrogen phosphate solution?
$\mathrm{Pka}_1=2.1, \mathrm{Pka}_2=7.2, \log 2=0.3$

questions.q:
A sparingly soluble salt MX is dissolved in water to prepare 1 lit. saturated solution. $\mathrm{Now} 10^{-6} \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{NaX}$ is added into this. Conductivity of this solution is $29 \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{~S} / \mathrm{m}$. If $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{sP}}$ of MX is $\mathrm{a} \times 10^{-\mathrm{b}}$ then find value of $(\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b})$ ?
Given $\quad \lambda_{\mathrm{x}^{-}}^0=4 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~S} \mathrm{~m}^2 \mathrm{~mol}^{-}$
$\lambda_{\mathrm{Na}^{+}}^0=5 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~S} \mathrm{~m}^2 \mathrm{~mol}^{-}$
$\lambda_{\mathrm{M}^{+}}^0=6 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~S} \mathrm{~m}^2 \mathrm{~mol}^{-}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
A mixture containing $x$ millimoles of NaOH and y milli moles of $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3$ requires.
(A) 4 ml of 1 N HCl using phenolphthalien indicator
(B) 5 ml of 1 N HCl using methyl orange indicator

The value of $x / y$ will be
questions.solution: From Eq. (1) and Eq. (2) we get $\begin{aligned} & y=1 \\ & x=3 \\ & \Rightarrow x / y=3\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: 5.35 g of a salt ACl (of weak base AOH ) is dissolved in 250 ml of solution. The pH of the resultant solution was found to be 4.827 . Find the ionic radius of $\mathrm{A}^{+}$and $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$if ACl forms CsCl type crystal having $2.2 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{cm}^3$. Given $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{b}(\mathrm{AOH})}=1.8 \times 10^{-5}, \frac{\mathrm{r}^{+}}{\mathrm{r}^{-}}=0.732$ for this cell unit (mark the anser in $Å$)
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{ACl}(\mathrm{s}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{A}^{+}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{Cl}^{-}(\mathrm{aq})$
$\mathrm{A}^{+}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{AOH}(\mathrm{aq})^{-}+\mathrm{H}^{+}(\mathrm{aq})$
At equilibrium $\quad \mathrm{C}(1-\alpha) \quad \mathrm{C} \alpha \quad \mathrm{C} \alpha$
For salts of weak base and strong acid, $\alpha=\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{w}}}{\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{b}}}}$
$\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]=\mathrm{C} \alpha=\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{w}} \times \mathrm{C}}{\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{b}}}}$
$10^{-4.827}=\sqrt{\frac{1 \times 10^{-4}}{1.8 \times 10^{-5}} \times \frac{5.35 \times 1000}{M \times 250}}$
$M=53.5$
For CsCl type structure
$\rho=\frac{z M}{a^3 \times 6.023 \times 10^{23}}$
$\mathrm{a}=\left[\frac{1 \times 53.5}{2.2 \times 6.023 \times 10^{23}}\right]^{1 / 3}=3.43 Å$
$r_A^{+}+r_{C r}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \times a=0.866 \mathrm{a}=0.866 \times 3.43 Å=2.97 Å$
$\because \frac{r_{A^{+}}}{r_{C-}}=0.732$
On solving $r_{A^{+}}=1.255 Å \Rightarrow r_{C l^{-}}=\mathbf{1 . 7 1 5} Å$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
